concepts.title: Banach and Hilbert spaces
concepts.blurb: Infinite-dimensional normed spaces provide the analytic home for operators and PDEs.

concepts.title: Bounded linear operators and the operator norm
concepts.blurb: B(X,Y) is Banach under the operator norm; for linear maps bounded = continuous = Lipschitz, and the norm is the largest singular value in the matrix case.

concepts.title: Bounded operators and adjoints
concepts.blurb: Linear operators on Hilbert spaces admit operator norms, adjoints, and projection geometry.

concepts.title: Adjoint operator on Hilbert space
concepts.blurb: Self-adjoint, normal, and unitary operators are detected through identities between T and T*; Pauli matrices make the distinctions concrete in 2×2.

concepts.title: The big four theorems
concepts.blurb: Hahn–Banach, Open Mapping, Closed Graph, and Banach–Steinhaus organize Banach-space analysis via Baire category and Zorn's lemma.

concepts.title: Compact operators and Fredholm alternative
concepts.blurb: Compact operators send bounded sets to precompact sets; Riesz–Schauder gives a discrete spectrum accumulating only at 0 and the Fredholm alternative for T−λI.

concepts.title: Spectral theorem viewpoint
concepts.blurb: Self-adjoint and normal operators are analyzed through spectral decompositions.

concepts.title: Spectrum classification: point, continuous, residual
concepts.blurb: The spectrum decomposes by which way T−λ fails to be invertible: not injective (point), dense-range-but-not-closed (continuous), or non-dense range (residual).

concepts.title: Riesz representation theorem
concepts.blurb: Every bounded linear functional on a Hilbert space is inner product with a unique vector; on Lᵖ it is integration against an Lᵩ density.

concepts.title: Weak and weak-* topologies
concepts.blurb: Coarsen the topology so dual pairings become continuous; Banach–Alaoglu then makes the unit ball of the dual weak-* compact.

concepts.title: Reflexivity
concepts.blurb: X is reflexive when the canonical embedding X → X** is surjective; Lᵖ (1<p<∞) and Hilbert spaces qualify; L¹, L∞, C[0,1], c₀ do not.

concepts.title: Distributions and test functions
concepts.blurb: The dual of Cc^∞ is the space of distributions — delta, principal value, tempered — extending differentiation and Fourier analysis beyond functions.
